title: phlox andicola subsp. andicola
intro: Phlox andicola subsp. andicola, a plant species of the phlox genus, is a type of flowering plant native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phlox andicola subsp. andicola include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area.
